notes: Antigravity background subagent, UNBLOCKED by subagent linking. Unified-rule spec: the parent dispatches a fire-and-forget background child via invoke_subagent and FINISHES ITS OWN REPLY (terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE no-tool = end_turn) while the detached child keeps writing its own brain/<child>/ transcript, yet the SESSION stays working because reevaluateParent holds it on hasActiveChildren; SubagentCount(parent) INCLUDES the live child. working→ready ONLY after the last background child drains. SAME daemon mechanism as foreground-subagent — the DISTINGUISHING observable is the timing: the parent's end_turn lands BEFORE the child drains. FAITHFULNESS GATE: child_link must precede child_drain, and the parent must NOT go ready between child_link and child_drain (intermediate_working invariant); if the parent went ready while the child was alive that is a held-working regression; if the parent only ended AFTER blocking on the child inline that is a foreground clone, refuted by the background dispatch + report-immediately prompt. The background child has no own agy process (pid=0, transcript-first) — assert ParentSessionID linkage + held-working, not a child PID. Model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> ('Gemini 3.1 Pro'). Tokens/cost NOT in the antigravity JSONL.

text: Parent conv-id session appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written (single-birth, no proc-<PID> presession)
The dispatch prompt drives the parent ready → working
The background subagent's own conversation transcript materializes at brain/<child>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState for the detached child

AntigravityParentConvID resolves the parent via its INVOKE_SUBAGENT step; detector emits parent_linked binding the background child.session_id → parent.session_id; SubagentCount(parent) now includes the live child

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: The parent's OWN reply has finished (end_turn) yet the SESSION stays working because the detached background child is still alive — the session does NOT go ready when the parent's reply ends (reevaluateParent / hasActiveChildren)
The background child finishes its work and its session transitions working → ready (SubagentCount drops toward 0)
Only AFTER the last background child drains does the parent session transition working → ready
